_comment: Machine-checkable handshake manifest for the Lead Developer skills. The authoritative source for check_handshakes.py. Every cross-skill state-file token is listed with the skills that WRITE it and the skills that READ it, plus a `grep` string that must literally appear in each of those skills' SKILL.md. The linter fails if: a token has no writer; a token has a reader-less write that isn't on the intentional allowlist; a declared writer/reader doesn't actually mention the token (drift); or a dual-owner seam has no first_owner. Keep this in sync with STATE-FILES.md. Built 2026-07-02 from the 26-agent handshake map.

tokens.note: Every skill locks onto the live copy first; all carry a write-if-missing fallback. First owner: readiness-check.
M2 fix: which branch is production (not assumed `main`). readiness-check records; ship-change + release publish to it.
The `## Logins & data` inventory in stack.md. go-live-check scopes its pillars off it; emergency-plan derives the break-list; handoff seeds the treasure hunt.
The crossroad/closed-shop mode + lever back to normal. ship-change consumes it too but via `display mode` wording.
Witnessed-restore proof. go-live-check re-proves under real data; emergency-plan/handoff carry the proof forward.
H1 fix: safety-net sets up a place to keep the keys; handoff assembles against it (map-not-vault).
safety-net.md's dated deferral ledger, 🔴 first. Re-surfaced by every downstream skill. (go-live-check also keeps its own Still-open ledger.)
staging.md marker: is the practice copy up. Gate for qa-harness / emergency-plan Part 2 / the release-foundation felt lap.
Seam: ship-change owns/captures it; qa-harness appends; go-live-check may fallback-capture (B12 — should write back to staging.md).
The plain-English version log + rollback entries. qa-harness now reads it for the regression intake (H2).
Proven checks + testability class + re-prove trigger + regression-covered stamps.
H2 + B16 fix: producers flag a break to become a permanent check; qa-harness's Step 0.4 intake ingests it and flips it to regression-covered.
The closed state of the loop. qa-harness writes it into the source file + its own row; producers' Step-0.4 scan skips it.
H3 fix: emergency-plan defers Part 2 with a dated tripwire; readiness-check (warm re-run) + ship-change (step zero) surface it stale.
H3 fix: emergency-plan's staleness date; surfaced by readiness-check + ship-change.
H3 fix: go-live-check's monitors-rot tripwire; surfaced by readiness-check + ship-change.
Seam: release-foundation creates the chart; ship-change adds parked-feature rows; release marks shipped / rolls dates.
Seam: release-foundation creates; release appends; either adopts-not-clobbers an existing one. handoff bundles it.
The derived emergency card PDF; handoff folds it into the recover section + first-day guide.
break-list / contact tree / run-history. qa-harness reads it for the regression intake (H2); handoff assembles it.

verdict + builder-confirmed proofs + wired alarms + regression-wanted. emergency-plan checks wired alarms before duplicating; qa-harness reads regression-wanted. (handoff reaches go-live status transitively via emergency-plan.md, not by reading go-live-check.md directly.)

intentional_write_no_read.reason: Read by the builder's ambient AI every session, not by a sibling skill. That IS the intended reader.
AI-memory rule for the everyday assistant; load-bearing mechanism is the reminder + chart.
AI-memory contract for a plain-chat panic session; handoff carries the leash via the PDF card, not this file (B3 refuted).
AI-memory rule (every change through the loop); ambient AI is the reader.
Terminal capstone record; nothing downstream by design.
Terminal deliverable for a human operator with no repo access.
Builder-facing future-task note in the cheat sheet.
Human-facing record; qa-harness re-derives realistic state from stack.md operating-mode (B4 — acceptable).

seams.rule: release-foundation creates; release appends; whichever finds an existing one adopts-and-appends, never recreates.
ship-change is the single source of truth; qa-harness appends named flows; go-live-check's fallback-capture should write back to staging.md, not diverge (B12).
go-live-check owns/wires it; emergency-plan Part-2c checks go-live-check.md first and does not duplicate; records back as wired+proven.
emergency-plan authors it; it ships through ship-change's loop and advances ship-change's restore point. Authorship = emergency-plan; publish side-effect = ship-change.
go-live-check proves it once, now; qa-harness makes it a permanent test. A go-live FAIL seeds a qa-harness test via a regression-wanted line (B16).
release-foundation creates; ship-change adds parked rows; release marks shipped / rolls dates.
readiness-check owns it; safety-net only writes it as a fallback when the front door was skipped.
